CAVEMAN MODE ACTIVE — level: full

Respond terse like smart caveman. All technical substance stay. Only fluff die.

## Persistence

ACTIVE EVERY RESPONSE. No revert after many turns. No filler drift. Still active if unsure. Off only: "stop caveman" / "normal mode".

Default: **full**. Switch: `/caveman lite|full|ultra`.

## Rules

Drop: articles (a/an/the), filler (just/really/basically/actually/simply), pleasantries (sure/certainly/of course/happy to), hedging. Fragments OK. Short synonyms (big not extensive, fix not "implement a solution for"). Technical terms exact. Code blocks unchanged. Errors quoted exact.

Pattern: `[thing] [action] [reason]. [next step].`

Not: "Sure! I'd be happy to help you with that. The issue you're experiencing is likely caused by..."
Yes: "Bug in auth middleware. Token expiry check use `<` not `<=`. Fix:"

## Intensity

| Level | What change |
|-------|------------|
| **full** | Drop articles, fragments OK, short synonyms. Classic caveman |

Example — "Why React component re-render?"
- full: "New object ref each render. Inline object prop = new ref = re-render. Wrap in `useMemo`."

Example — "Explain database connection pooling."
- full: "Pool reuse open DB connections. No new connection per request. Skip handshake overhead."

## Auto-Clarity

Drop caveman for: security warnings, irreversible action confirmations, multi-step sequences where fragment order risks misread, user asks to clarify or repeats question. Resume caveman after clear part done.

Example — destructive op:
> **Warning:** This will permanently delete all rows in the `users` table and cannot be undone.
> ```sql
> DROP TABLE users;
> ```
> Caveman resume. Verify backup exist first.

## Boundaries

Code/commits/PRs: write normal. "stop caveman" or "normal mode": revert. Level persist until changed or session end.

116	
117	    func setupScreenMonitoring() {
118	        let nc = NSWorkspace.shared.notificationCenter
119	        nc.addObserver(self, selector: #selector(screenWoke),
120	                       name: NSWorkspace.screensDidWakeNotification, object: nil)
121	        nc.addObserver(self, selector: #selector(screenSlept),
122	                       name: NSWorkspace.screensDidSleepNotification, object: nil)
123	
124	        let dnc = DistributedNotificationCenter.default()
125	        dnc.addObserver(self, selector: #selector(screensaverStarted),
126	                        name: .init("com.apple.screensaver.didstart"), object: nil)
127	        dnc.addObserver(self, selector: #selector(screensaverStopped),
128	                        name: .init("com.apple.screensaver.didstop"), object: nil)
129	    }
130	
131	    @objc func screenWoke() {
132	        recordScreenEvent(.on)
133	        if projectTimer == nil { scheduleProjectTimer() }
134	        guard !isLoggedOut else { return }
135	        // Prompt on lid-open return if absent long enough (screensaver path handles its own)
136	        let sinceLastPrompt = Date().timeIntervalSince(lastPromptShown ?? .distantPast)
137	        if sinceLastPrompt > promptInterval {
138	            pendingPromptEntryTime = screenSleepTime
139	            askForProject(isAutoPrompt: true)
140	        }
141	    }
142	    @objc func screenSlept() {
143	        let screensaverWasRunning = screensaverStartTime != nil
144	        // If screensaver was running, use its start time as the effective departure (work window already closed then)
145	        screenSleepTime = screensaverStartTime ?? Date()
146	        screensaverStartTime = nil
147	        // Only stamp if screensaver wasn't already running — screensaverStarted already closed the work window
148	        if !screensaverWasRunning && !currentProject.isEmpty { recordProjectEntry(currentProject) }
149	        recordScreenEvent(.off)
150	    }
151	    @objc func screensaverStarted() {
152	        let now = Date()
153	        screensaverStartTime = now
154	        if !currentProject.isEmpty { recordProjectEntry(currentProject, at: now) }  // stamp current project at screensaver start
155	        recordScreenEvent(.screensaverOn)
156	    }
157	    @objc func screensaverStopped() {
158	        recordScreenEvent(.screensaverOff)
159	        guard !isLoggedOut else { screensaverStartTime = nil; return }
160	        if let start = screensaverStartTime {
161	            let absence = Date().timeIntervalSince(start)
162	            screensaverStartTime = nil
163	            if absence > 60 {
164	                pendingPromptEntryTime = start
165	                // Delay slightly — screen layout isn't stable the instant the screensaver ends
166	                DispatchQueue.main.asyncAfter(deadline: .now() + 0.5) {
167	                    self.askForProject(isAutoPrompt: true)
168	                }
169	            }
170	        }
171	    }

175	    func setupHeartbeat() {
176	        heartbeatTimer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: AppDelegate.heartbeatInterval, repeats: true) { [weak self] _ in
177	            guard let self else { return }
178	            self.handleStaleHeartbeat()
179	            self.recordHeartbeat()
180	        }

184	    private func recordHeartbeat() {
185	        try? "\(Date().timeIntervalSince1970)".data(using: .utf8)?.write(to: heartbeatFileURL, options: .atomic)
186	    }
187	
188	    private func handleStaleHeartbeat() {
189	        guard let data = try? Data(contentsOf: heartbeatFileURL),
190	              let timeStr = String(data: data, encoding: .utf8),
191	              let lastHeartbeatTime = TimeInterval(timeStr) else {
192	            return
193	        }
194	        let lastHeartbeat = Date(timeIntervalSince1970: lastHeartbeatTime)
195	
196	        let threshold = AppDelegate.heartbeatStaleThreshold
197	        let age = Date().timeIntervalSince(lastHeartbeat)
198	
199	        guard age > threshold else { return }
200	
201	        recordScreenEvent(.off, at: lastHeartbeat)
202	        recordScreenEvent(.on)
203	    }

205	    func recordScreenEvent(_ kind: ScreenEvent.Kind, at time: Date = Date()) {
206	        var data = loadData()
207	        let todayEvents = data.screenEvents.filter { Calendar.current.isDateInToday($0.time) }
208	        if let last = todayEvents.last, last.kind == kind { return }
209	        data.screenEvents.append(ScreenEvent(kind: kind, time: time))
210	        saveData(data)
211	        eventLog.append(at: time, eventType: kind.rawValue, eventSource: "computer", projectName: "-")
212	    }

228	    // Returns true when Keynote/Zoom/Teams/etc. is holding a display-sleep prevention
229	    // assertion — the reliable cross-version signal that a presentation is in progress.
230	    func isPresentationActive() -> Bool {
231	        var byProcess: Unmanaged<CFDictionary>?
232	        guard IOPMCopyAssertionsByProcess(&byProcess) == kIOReturnSuccess,
233	              let dict = byProcess?.takeRetainedValue() as? [String: [[String: Any]]] else {
234	            return false
235	        }
236	        return dict.values.contains { assertions in
237	            assertions.contains { ($0["AssertType"] as? String) == "PreventUserIdleDisplaySleep" }
238	        }
239	    }

257	    func screensaverTimeout() -> TimeInterval {
258	        // idleTime first (active setting), lastDelayTime as fallback (last known before disabled)
259	        for key in ["idleTime", "lastDelayTime"] {
260	            if let val = CFPreferencesCopyValue(
261	                key as CFString,
262	                "com.apple.screensaver" as CFString,
263	                kCFPreferencesCurrentUser,
264	                kCFPreferencesCurrentHost
265	            ) as? NSNumber, val.intValue > 0 {
266	                return val.doubleValue
267	            }
268	        }
269	        return 0
270	    }

390	    func addDayItems(to menu: NSMenu, date: Date, appData: AppData, timeFmt: DateFormatter) {
391	        let cal = Calendar.current
392	        let dayScreenEvents = appData.screenEvents.filter { cal.isDate($0.time, inSameDayAs: date) }
393	        let dayProjects = appData.projectEntries
394	            .filter { cal.isDate($0.time, inSameDayAs: date) && !$0.project.hasPrefix("~") }
395	            .sorted { $0.time < $1.time }
396	        let firstOnTime = dayScreenEvents.first(where: { $0.kind == .on || $0.kind == .screensaverOff })?.time
397	
398	        let now = Date()
399	        let spans = TimeCalculations.buildTimeSpans(from: dayScreenEvents, projectEntries: dayProjects, now: now)
400	        let worked = TimeCalculations.workedTime(spans: spans, entries: dayProjects, firstOnTime: firstOnTime, now: now)
401	
402	        let totalItem = NSMenuItem(title: "Worked: \(formatDuration(worked))", action: nil, keyEquivalent: "")
403	        totalItem.isEnabled = false
404	        menu.addItem(totalItem)
405	        menu.addItem(.separator())
406	        if spans.isEmpty {
407	            let item = NSMenuItem(title: "No events yet", action: nil, keyEquivalent: "")
408	            item.isEnabled = false
409	            menu.addItem(item)
410	        } else {
411	            for span in spans {
412	                let spanEnd = span.end ?? Date()
413	                guard Int(spanEnd.timeIntervalSince(span.start) / 60) > 0 else { continue }
414	                let startStr = timeFmt.string(from: span.start)
415	                let endStr   = span.isActive ? "now" : timeFmt.string(from: span.end!)
416	                let dur      = formatDuration(spanEnd.timeIntervalSince(span.start))
417	                let title    = "\(startStr) – \(endStr)  (\(dur))"
418	
419	                let reportedInSpan = Set(dayProjects
420	                    .filter { $0.time >= span.start && $0.time <= spanEnd }
421	                    .map { $0.project })
422	
423	                let durations = TimeCalculations.projectDurations(
424	                    entries: dayProjects,
425	                    firstOnTime: firstOnTime,
426	                    spanStart: span.start,
427	                    spanEnd: spanEnd
428	                ).filter { reportedInSpan.contains($0.project) }
429	
430	                let item = NSMenuItem(title: title, action: nil, keyEquivalent: "")
431	                let totalAttributed = durations.reduce(0.0) { $0 + $1.duration }
432	                let diff = spanEnd.timeIntervalSince(span.start) - totalAttributed
433	
434	                if !durations.isEmpty || diff > 60 {
435	                    let submenu = NSMenu()
436	                    for pd in durations {
437	                        let pItem = NSMenuItem(title: "\(pd.project)  \(formatDuration(pd.duration))", action: nil, keyEquivalent: "")
438	                        pItem.isEnabled = false
439	                        submenu.addItem(pItem)
440	                    }
441	                    if diff > 60 {
442	                        if !durations.isEmpty { submenu.addItem(.separator()) }
443	                        let diffItem = NSMenuItem(title: "diff  \(formatDuration(diff))", action: nil, keyEquivalent: "")
444	                        diffItem.isEnabled = false
445	                        submenu.addItem(diffItem)
446	                    }
447	                    item.submenu = submenu
448	                } else {
449	                    item.isEnabled = false
450	                }
451	                menu.addItem(item)
452	            }
453	        }
454	    }

474	    func scheduleProjectTimer() {
475	        guard !isLoggedOut else { return }
476	        projectTimer?.invalidate()
477	        nextPromptDate = Date().addingTimeInterval(promptInterval)
478	        projectTimer = Timer.scheduledTimer(withTimeInterval: promptInterval, repeats: false) { [weak self] _ in
479	            guard let self else { return }
480	            let data = self.loadData()
481	            let userActive = !(data.screenEvents.filter { Calendar.current.isDateInToday($0.time) }.last?.kind.isAway ?? false)
482	            if userActive {
483	                self.askForProject(isAutoPrompt: true)
484	            } else {
485	                // Screen off — skip popup but still schedule next
486	                self.scheduleProjectTimer()
487	            }
488	        }
489	        RunLoop.main.add(projectTimer!, forMode: .common)
490	    }

492	    func recentProjects() -> [String] {
493	        let entries = loadData().projectEntries
494	        var seen = Set<String>()
495	        var result: [String] = []
496	        for entry in entries.reversed() {
497	            if seen.insert(entry.project).inserted {

499	                if result.count >= 100 { break }
500	            }
501	        }

508	
509	    func askForProject(isAutoPrompt: Bool) {
510	        DispatchQueue.main.async {
511	            // Skip if Focus/DND or a presentation is active — reschedule and try again later
512	            if isAutoPrompt && (self.isFocusActive || self.isPresentationActive()) {
513	                self.scheduleProjectTimer()
514	                return
515	            }
516	
517	            // Don't stack prompts — but resurface existing window if manually triggered
518	            guard self.promptWindow == nil else {
519	                if !isAutoPrompt { self.promptWindow?.show() }
520	                else { self.scheduleProjectTimer() }
521	                return
522	            }
523	
524	            let window = ProjectPromptWindow(
525	                currentProject: self.currentProject,
526	                recentProjects: self.recentProjects()
527	            )
528	            self.promptWindow = window
529	            let awayTime = self.pendingPromptEntryTime  // when user left — nil if manually triggered
530	            let returnTime = Date()
531	
532	            window.onSave = { [weak self] val in
533	                guard let self else { return }
534	                self.currentProject = val
535	                self.recordProjectEntry(val, at: returnTime)  // project starts at return, not departure
536	            }
537	
538	            window.onBreak = { [weak self] in
539	                guard let self else { return }
540	                self.currentProject = "Break"
541	                self.recordProjectEntry("Break", at: awayTime ?? returnTime)  // break covers the absence
542	            }
543	
544	            window.onDismiss = { [weak self] in
545	                guard let self else { return }
546	                self.pendingPromptEntryTime = nil
547	                self.promptWindow = nil
548	                if isAutoPrompt { self.scheduleProjectTimer() }
549	            }
550	
551	            self.lastPromptShown = Date()
552	            window.show(startActive: !isAutoPrompt)
553	        }
554	    }

563	    func timeAgo(from date: Date) -> String {
564	        let seconds = Int(Date().timeIntervalSince(date))
565	        if seconds < 60   { return "just now" }
566	        if seconds < 3600 { return "\(seconds / 60) min ago" }
567	        let h = seconds / 3600
568	        let m = (seconds % 3600) / 60
569	        return m > 0 ? "\(h)h \(m)m ago" : "\(h)h ago"
570	    }
571	
572	    func timeUntil(_ date: Date) -> String {
573	        let seconds = Int(date.timeIntervalSinceNow)
574	        guard seconds > 0 else { return "soon" }
575	        let timeFmt = DateFormatter()
576	        timeFmt.timeStyle = .short
577	        timeFmt.dateStyle = .none
578	        let clock = timeFmt.string(from: date)
579	        if seconds < 60   { return "in \(seconds)s (\(clock))" }
580	        if seconds < 3600 { return "in \(seconds / 60) min (\(clock))" }
581	        let h = seconds / 3600
582	        let m = (seconds % 3600) / 60
583	        let dur = m > 0 ? "\(h)h \(m)m" : "\(h)h"
584	        return "in \(dur) (\(clock))"
585	    }

589	    @objc func exportCSV() {
590	        let csv = buildCSV()
591	        DispatchQueue.main.async {
592	            NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)
593	            let panel = NSSavePanel()
594	            panel.allowedContentTypes = [.commaSeparatedText]
595	            panel.nameFieldStringValue = "BarTimeTracker_export.csv"
596	            panel.begin { response in
597	                guard response == .OK, let url = panel.url else { return }
598	                try? csv.write(to: url, atomically: true, encoding: .utf8)
599	            }
600	        }
601	    }
602	
603	    func buildCSV() -> String {
604	        let appData = loadData()
605	        let cal = Calendar.current
606	        let dateFmt = DateFormatter()
607	        dateFmt.dateFormat = "yyyy-MM-dd"
608	        let timeFmt = DateFormatter()
609	        timeFmt.dateFormat = "HH:mm"
610	
611	        func csvField(_ s: String) -> String {
612	            s.contains(";") || s.contains("\"") || s.contains("\n")
613	                ? "\"\(s.replacingOccurrences(of: "\"", with: "\"\""))\"" : s
614	        }
615	
616	        struct RawRow {
617	            let date: String
618	            var start: Date
619	            var end: Date
620	            let project: String
621	        }
622	
623	        var rows: [RawRow] = []
624	
625	        let allDates = (appData.screenEvents.map(\.time) + appData.projectEntries.map(\.time))
626	            .map { cal.startOfDay(for: $0) }
627	        let days = Array(Set(allDates)).sorted()
628	
629	        for day in days {
630	            let dayScreenEvents = appData.screenEvents.filter { cal.isDate($0.time, inSameDayAs: day) }
631	            let dayProjects = appData.projectEntries
632	                .filter { cal.isDate($0.time, inSameDayAs: day) && !$0.project.hasPrefix("~") }
633	                .sorted { $0.time < $1.time }
634	            let firstOnTime = dayScreenEvents.first(where: { $0.kind == .on || $0.kind == .screensaverOff })?.time
635	            let dayStart = firstOnTime ?? day
636	            let spans = TimeCalculations.buildTimeSpans(from: dayScreenEvents, projectEntries: dayProjects, now: Date())
637	            let dateStr = dateFmt.string(from: day)
638	
639	            func addRow(_ start: Date, _ end: Date, _ project: String) {
640	                guard end > start, Int(end.timeIntervalSince(start) / 60) > 0 else { return }
641	                rows.append(RawRow(date: dateStr, start: start, end: end, project: project))
642	            }
643	
644	            for span in spans {
645	                let spanEnd = span.end ?? Date()
646	                let indicesInSpan = dayProjects.indices.filter {
647	                    dayProjects[$0].time > span.start && dayProjects[$0].time <= spanEnd
648	                }
649	
650	                if indicesInSpan.isEmpty {
651	                    addRow(span.start, spanEnd, "")
652	                    continue
653	                }
654	
655	                var coveredUpTo = span.start
656	
657	                for idx in indicesInSpan {
658	                    let entry = dayProjects[idx]
659	                    let claimFrom = idx == 0 ? dayStart : dayProjects[idx - 1].time
660	                    let rowStart = max(claimFrom, span.start)
661	                    let rowEnd = entry.time
662	
663	                    if rowStart > coveredUpTo { addRow(coveredUpTo, rowStart, "") }
664	                    if entry.project != "Break" { addRow(rowStart, rowEnd, entry.project) }
665	                    coveredUpTo = rowEnd
666	                }
667	
668	                if let lastIdx = indicesInSpan.last {
669	                    let lastEntry = dayProjects[lastIdx]
670	                    let rowStart = max(lastEntry.time, span.start)
671	                    if rowStart > coveredUpTo { addRow(coveredUpTo, rowStart, "") }
672	                    if lastEntry.project != "Break" { addRow(rowStart, spanEnd, lastEntry.project) }
673	                }
674	            }
675	        }
676	
677	        // Merge consecutive rows with the same date and project
678	        var merged: [RawRow] = []
679	        for row in rows {
680	            if var last = merged.last,
681	               last.date == row.date,
682	               last.project == row.project,
683	               last.end == row.start {
684	                last.end = row.end
685	                merged[merged.count - 1] = last
686	            } else {
687	                merged.append(row)
688	            }
689	        }
690	
691	        var lines = ["Date;Start time;End time;Duration (minutes);Project/Description"]
692	        for row in merged {
693	            let durMin = Int(row.end.timeIntervalSince(row.start) / 60)
694	            lines.append("\(row.date);\(timeFmt.string(from: row.start));\(timeFmt.string(from: row.end));\(durMin);\(csvField(row.project))")
695	        }
696	        return lines.joined(separator: "\n")
697	    }

import XCTest
import Foundation
@testable import BarTimeTrackerCore

// Tests use real events.csv data (snapshot embedded below) as input.
// Expected values computed by running the current algorithm against the CSV.
// To update: edit the expected values then fix the algorithm until tests pass.
//
// Timezone: Europe/Stockholm (CEST = UTC+2 in summer)
// "now" per day: last screen event on that day + 1s (makes closed days fully deterministic)

final class EventLogCalculationTests: XCTestCase {

    private static let tz = TimeZone(identifier: "Europe/Stockholm")!

    private func XCTAssertDuration(
        _ actual: TimeInterval, _ expected: TimeInterval,
        tolerance: TimeInterval = 60,
        _ label: String = "",
        file: StaticString = #file, line: UInt = #line
    ) {
        XCTAssertEqual(actual, expected, accuracy: tolerance, label, file: file, line: line)
    }

    private func analyze(csv: String, now: Date) -> (
        spans: [TimeSpan], worked: TimeInterval, projects: [String: TimeInterval]
    ) {
        let (screenEvents, projectEntries) = EventLogParser.parse(csv: csv, timeZone: Self.tz)
        let entries = projectEntries.filter { !$0.project.hasPrefix("~") }
        let firstOn = screenEvents.first(where: { $0.kind == .on || $0.kind == .screensaverOff })?.time
        let spans = TimeCalculations.buildTimeSpans(from: screenEvents, projectEntries: entries, now: now)
        let worked = TimeCalculations.workedTime(spans: spans, entries: entries, firstOnTime: firstOn, now: now)
        let totals = TimeCalculations.dailyProjectTotals(spans: spans, entries: entries, firstOnTime: firstOn, now: now)
        return (spans, worked, Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: totals.map { ($0.project, $0.duration) }))
    }

    private func date(_ s: String) -> Date {
        let f = DateFormatter()
        f.dateFormat = "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
        f.timeZone = Self.tz
        return f.date(from: s)!
    }

    // MARK: - May 28  (now = 20:40:27 local)
    // Short afternoon session: Librixer only, then a break-only evening span.
    // Expected: 2 spans, 54m 38s worked, Librixer 54m 38s.

    private let may28CSV = """
    date,time,event_type,event_source,project_name
    2026-05-28,16:07:27,on,computer,-

    2026-05-28,16:38:12,entry,user,Librixer
    2026-05-28,16:53:48,entry,user,Librixer
    2026-05-28,17:02:05,entry,user,Librixer
    2026-05-28,17:02:05,screensaverOn,computer,-
    2026-05-28,18:02:06,off,computer,-
    2026-05-28,19:40:03,on,computer,-
    2026-05-28,19:40:04,screensaverOff,computer,-
    2026-05-28,17:02:05,entry,user,Break
    2026-05-28,19:45:15,entry,user,Break
    2026-05-28,19:45:15,screensaverOn,computer,-
    2026-05-28,20:40:26,off,computer,-
    """

    func test_may28_spanCount() {
        let (spans, _, _) = analyze(csv: may28CSV, now: date("2026-05-28 20:40:27"))
        XCTAssertEqual(spans.count, 2)
    }

    func test_may28_allClosed() {
        let (spans, _, _) = analyze(csv: may28CSV, now: date("2026-05-28 20:40:27"))
        XCTAssertTrue(spans.allSatisfy { !$0.isActive })
    }

    func test_may28_workedTime() {
        let (_, worked, _) = analyze(csv: may28CSV, now: date("2026-05-28 20:40:27"))
        XCTAssertDuration(worked, 3278, "May 28 worked")
    }

    func test_may28_projectDurations() {
        let (_, _, projects) = analyze(csv: may28CSV, now: date("2026-05-28 20:40:27"))
        XCTAssertDuration(projects["Librixer"] ?? 0, 3278, "Librixer")
        XCTAssertNil(projects["Break"])

import XCTest
import Foundation
@testable import BarTimeTrackerCore

// Tests use real events.csv data (snapshot embedded below) as input.
// Expected values computed by running the current algorithm against the CSV.
// To update: edit the expected values then fix the algorithm until tests pass.
//
// Timezone: Europe/Stockholm (CEST = UTC+2 in summer)
// "now" per day: last screen event on that day + 1s (makes closed days fully deterministic)

final class EventLogCalculationTests: XCTestCase {

    private static let tz = TimeZone(identifier: "Europe/Stockholm")!

    private func XCTAssertDuration(
        _ actual: TimeInterval, _ expected: TimeInterval,
        tolerance: TimeInterval = 60,
        _ label: String = "",
        file: StaticString = #file, line: UInt = #line
    ) {
        XCTAssertEqual(actual, expected, accuracy: tolerance, label, file: file, line: line)
    }

    private func analyze(csv: String, now: Date) -> (
        spans: [TimeSpan], worked: TimeInterval, projects: [String: TimeInterval]
    ) {
        let (screenEvents, projectEntries) = EventLogParser.parse(csv: csv, timeZone: Self.tz)
        let entries = projectEntries.filter { !$0.project.hasPrefix("~") }
        let firstOn = screenEvents.first(where: { $0.kind == .on || $0.kind == .screensaverOff })?.time
        let spans = TimeCalculations.buildTimeSpans(from: screenEvents, projectEntries: entries, now: now)
        let worked = TimeCalculations.workedTime(spans: spans, entries: entries, firstOnTime: firstOn, now: now)
        let totals = TimeCalculations.dailyProjectTotals(spans: spans, entries: entries, firstOnTime: firstOn, now: now)
        return (spans, worked, Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: totals.map { ($0.project, $0.duration) }))
    }

    private func date(_ s: String) -> Date {
        let f = DateFormatter()
        f.dateFormat = "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
        f.timeZone = Self.tz
        return f.date(from: s)!
    }

8	//
9	// Timezone: Europe/Stockholm (CEST = UTC+2 in summer)
10	// "now" per day: last screen event on that day + 1s (makes closed days fully deterministic)
11	
12	final class EventLogCalculationTests: XCTestCase {
13	
14	    private static let tz = TimeZone(identifier: "Europe/Stockholm")!
15	
16	    private func XCTAssertDuration(
17	        _ actual: TimeInterval, _ expected: TimeInterval,
18	        tolerance: TimeInterval = 60,
19	        _ label: String = "",
20	        file: StaticString = #file, line: UInt = #line
21	    ) {
22	        XCTAssertEqual(actual, expected, accuracy: tolerance, label, file: file, line: line)
23	    }
24	
25	    private func analyze(csv: String, now: Date) -> (
26	        spans: [TimeSpan], worked: TimeInterval, projects: [String: TimeInterval]
27	    ) {
28	        let (screenEvents, projectEntries) = EventLogParser.parse(csv: csv, timeZone: Self.tz)
29	        let entries = projectEntries.filter { !$0.project.hasPrefix("~") }
30	        let firstOn = screenEvents.first(where: { $0.kind == .on || $0.kind == .screensaverOff })?.time
31	        let spans = TimeCalculations.buildTimeSpans(from: screenEvents, projectEntries: entries, now: now)
32	        let worked = TimeCalculations.workedTime(spans: spans, entries: entries, firstOnTime: firstOn, now: now)
33	        let totals = TimeCalculations.dailyProjectTotals(spans: spans, entries: entries, firstOnTime: firstOn, now: now)
34	        return (spans, worked, Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: totals.map { ($0.project, $0.duration) }))
35	    }
36	
37	    private func date(_ s: String) -> Date {
38	        let f = DateFormatter()
39	        f.dateFormat = "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
40	        f.timeZone = Self.tz
41	        return f.date(from: s)!
42	    }

import XCTest
import Foundation
@testable import BarTimeTrackerCore

// Tests use real events.csv data (snapshot embedded below) as input.
// Expected values computed by running the current algorithm against the CSV.
// To update: edit the expected values then fix the algorithm until tests pass.
//
// Timezone: Europe/Stockholm (CEST = UTC+2 in summer)
// "now" per day: last screen event on that day + 1s (makes closed days fully deterministic)

final class EventLogCalculationTests: XCTestCase {

    private static let tz = TimeZone(identifier: "Europe/Stockholm")!

    private func XCTAssertDuration(
        _ actual: TimeInterval, _ expected: TimeInterval,
        tolerance: TimeInterval = 60,
        _ label: String = "",
        file: StaticString = #file, line: UInt = #line
    ) {
        XCTAssertEqual(actual, expected, accuracy: tolerance, label, file: file, line: line)
    }

    private func analyze(csv: String, now: Date) -> (
        spans: [TimeSpan], worked: TimeInterval, projects: [String: TimeInterval]
    ) {
        let (screenEvents, projectEntries) = EventLogParser.parse(csv: csv, timeZone: Self.tz)
        let entries = projectEntries.filter { !$0.project.hasPrefix("~") }
        let firstOn = screenEvents.first(where: { $0.kind == .on || $0.kind == .screensaverOff })?.time
        let spans = TimeCalculations.buildTimeSpans(from: screenEvents, projectEntries: entries, now: now)
        let worked = TimeCalculations.workedTime(spans: spans, entries: entries, firstOnTime: firstOn, now: now)
        let totals = TimeCalculations.dailyProjectTotals(spans: spans, entries: entries, firstOnTime: firstOn, now: now)
        return (spans, worked, Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: totals.map { ($0.project, $0.duration) }))
    }

    private func date(_ s: String) -> Date {
        let f = DateFormatter()
        f.dateFormat = "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"
        f.timeZone = Self.tz
        return f.date(from: s)!
    }

    // MARK: - May 28  (now = 20:40:27 local)
    // Short afternoon session: Librixer only, then a break-only evening span.
    // Expected: 2 spans, 54m 38s worked, Librixer 54m 38s.

    2026-05-28,17:02:05,entry,user,Librixer
    2026-05-28,17:02:05,screensaverOn,computer,-

    2026-05-28,19:40:03,on,computer,-

    2026-05-28,19:45:15,entry,user,Break
    2026-05-28,19:45:15,screensaverOn,computer,-
    2026-05-28,20:40:26,off,computer,-
    """

    func test_may28_spanCount() {
        let (spans, _, _) = analyze(csv: may28CSV, now: date("2026-05-28 20:40:27"))
        XCTAssertEqual(spans.count, 2)
    }

    func test_may28_allClosed() {
        let (spans, _, _) = analyze(csv: may28CSV, now: date("2026-05-28 20:40:27"))
        XCTAssertTrue(spans.allSatisfy { !$0.isActive })
    }

    func test_may28_workedTime() {
        let (_, worked, _) = analyze(csv: may28CSV, now: date("2026-05-28 20:40:27"))
        XCTAssertDuration(worked, 3278, "May 28 worked")
    }

        let (_, _, projects) = analyze(csv: may28CSV, now: date("2026-05-28 20:40:27"))

        XCTAssertNil(projects["Break"])
